summary: Soundboard capture of TC’s last show delivers razor‑sharp Jerry leads and standout performances of The Other One, High Time and Easy Wind, with solid recording quality despite minor tape cuts.
review: This 1/30/70 Vernon show is historically significant as Tom Constanten’s final Dead gig and the band’s last night before the bust.  Reviewers rave about the tight, atmospheric renditions of The Other One, Black Peter, High Time, Easy Wind and China Cat, noting Jerry Garcia’s razor‑sharp leads, while the soundboard tape is generally clear though it suffers a few audible cuts.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (8 of 10) give 4‑5 stars and praise both the musical performance and the sound quality; only a few note minor recording imperfections, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced sound with crisp instrument separation.  A couple of reviewers mention tape cuts (Cosmic Charlie) and that it isn’t as pristine as some SBD purists expect, but overall the recording is very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows from energetic openers like Casey Jones and Me & My Uncle into a mellow groove of Pigpen‑Jerry ballads, with seamless segues (e.g., Other One → Cosmic Charlie) that keep the momentum lively despite the looming bust.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers highlight Garcia’s razor‑sharp leads, perfect tone and atmospheric playing on High Time and other songs.
band_member_comments.Keys: Tom Constanten’s last appearance is noted; his playing is described as muddled at times but shines on China Cat and High Time.
